mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2019-11-15 indicates deep easing cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS < 1.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS MoM growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: demand slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS MoM declines exceed -2%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining below 102.0 combined with rising unemployment signals supply-side weakness and labor market deterioration, reducing equity valuations through lower growth expectations and higher risk premiums over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO < 102.0 AND UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.7435 and UNRATE at 6.3% as of 2019-11-15
regime_hint: supply contraction with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO and UNRATE shows Rank IC < 0.02 when tested as interaction term over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,000 thousand units combined with falling retail sales signals broad-based demand weakness across residential and consumer sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,072 thousand and RSAFS at 437,648 million as of 2019-11-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across housing and retail
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS) shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.5% signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression, which initially supports equity valuations but creates tail risk of policy reversal, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 236.254 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2019-11-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction of inflation and low rates shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which compresses earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.3% as of 2019-11-15 indicates elevated unemployment
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly combined with sustained payroll employment growth signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in late-cycle expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 100,000
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15,902.9 billion and PAYEMS at 138,463 thousand as of 2019-10-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized growth in output and employment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction of GDP and payroll growth shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
